title: Intraoperative recordings of medianus stimulation with low and high impedance ECoG

funding_references.funder_name: Alexander S. Onassis Public Benefit Foundation Scholarship
Alexander S. Onassis Public Benefit Foundation

description: This dataset comprises intraoperative intracranial EEG (ECoG) recordings from 10 patients undergoing brain tumor resection in the perirolandic region. The study compares fast ripple detection capabilities between low-impedance (3.4 kΩ) and high-impedance (6.9 kΩ) ECoG electrode contacts during median nerve somatosensory evoked potential (SEP) recordings. The dataset includes continuous raw ECoG data from bilateral 4-contact electrode strips recorded simultaneously at a stimulation rate of 4.7 Hz, enabling investigation of electrode impedance effects on high-frequency oscillation detection in clinical neurophysiology.
methods_description: Intraoperative ECoG recordings were obtained from two 4-contact electrode strips with different impedance characteristics (LoZ: 3.4 kΩ median impedance; HiZ: 6.9 kΩ median impedance) positioned over the hand area. Median nerve stimulation was delivered at 4.7 Hz. Data processing included high-pass filtering (200 Hz cutoff) for stimulus artifact detection, sweep definition with 50 ms post-stimulus recording length, artifact rejection (±100 µV amplitude threshold), and averaging of 100 sweeps. Frequency-domain analysis was performed using bandpass filtering in the N20 (30-300 Hz), fast ripple (250-500 Hz), and high-frequency oscillation (500-1000 Hz) bands.
